     The Numbers Business Report

Ender’s Game (2013)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $61,737,191Details
International Box Office $66,246,092
Worldwide Box Office $127,983,283
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $14,078,172
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $14,412,275
Total $28,490,447
Further financial details...

Synopsis

In the near future, a hostile alien race (called the Formics) have attacked Earth. If not for the legendary heroics of International Fleet Commander Mazer Rackham, all would have been lost. In preparation for the next attack, the highly esteemed Colonel Hyrum Graff and the International Military are training only the best young children to find the future Mazer. Ender Wiggin, a shy, but strategically brilliant boy is pulled out of his school to join the elite. Arriving at Battle School, Ender quickly and easily masters increasingly difficult war games, distinguishing himself and winning respect amongst his peers. Ender is soon ordained by Graff as the military's next great hope, resulting in his promotion to Command School. Once there, he's trained by Mazer Rackham himself, to lead his fellow soldiers into an epic battle that will determine the future of Earth and of the human race.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$27,017,351 (43.8% of total gross)
Legs:2.29 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:48.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$110,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,407 opening theaters/3,407 max. theaters, 4.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $90,817,985

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 1st, 2013 (Wide) by Lionsgate
November 1st, 2013 (IMAX) by Lionsgate
International Releases: October 25th, 2013 (Wide), released as Ender's Game (United Kingdom)
October 31st, 2013 (Wide), released as Ender's Game (Croatia)
October 31st, 2013 (Wide), released as Ender's Game (United Arab Emirates)
October 31st, 2013 (Wide), released as Ender's Game (Thailand)
November 1st, 2013 (Wide), released as Ender's Game (Vietnam)
Video Release: February 11th, 2014 by Lionsgate/Summit, released as Ender's Game
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some violence, sci-fi action and thematic material.
(Rating bulletin 2290, 9/18/2013)
Running Time: 114 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Motion Capture Performance
Film format: IMAX: DMR
Plot point: Child Prodigy, Coming of Age, New Guy/Girl in School, Surprise Twist
Social setting: Alien Invasion, Military Life
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Summit Entertainment, OddLot Entertainment, Chartoff Productions, Inc., Taleswapper, K/O Paper Products, Digital Domain
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

In the near future, a hostile alien race (called the Formics) have attacked Earth. If not for the legendary heroics of International Fleet Commander Mazer Rackham, all would have been lost. In preparation for the next attack, the highly esteemed Colonel Hyrum Graff and the International Military are training only the best young children to find the future Mazer. Ender Wiggin, a shy, but strategically brilliant boy is pulled out of his school to join the elite. Arriving at Battle School, Ender quickly and easily masters increasingly difficult war games, distinguishing himself and winning respect amongst his peers. Ender is soon ordained by Graff as the military's next great hope, resulting in his promotion to Command School. Once there, he's trained by Mazer Rackham himself, to lead his fellow soldiers into an epic battle that will determine the future of Earth and of the human race.
